description: Cross-language parity vectors for #2311 protocol contract hash. Python (claudemd-gen) and Rust (chorus-hooks protocol_contract) MUST produce identical digests on FIXED synthetic inputs — that proves the canonicalization ALGORITHM matches across languages. #3254: the old `live_core` fixture (which pinned the hash of the 13 live protocol-core fragments) was REMOVED — it was a content snapshot that went stale on every CLAUDE.md regen (e23de77→c6958c6e in one day) and cried wolf in the nightly. Whether the live doc matches its fragments is the RUNTIME guard's job (protocol_contract::check — self-relative: stamp vs freshly-computed fragment hash), not this unit test's. Add only fixed-byte fixtures here; never pin a live-content hash.
algorithm: sha256 over sorted entries; per entry: utf8(rel_path) || 0x00 || ascii(sha256_hex(content_bytes)) || 0x00

fixtures.name: empty
fixtures.description: Empty fragment set — canonical 'hash of nothing fed with the algorithm'. Both sides must agree that sha256() over no input yields the same digest.

fixtures.name: known_content
fixtures.description: Two synthetic fragments with fixed bytes. If canonicalization drifts (encoding, separator, sort order) this hash changes and both Python and Rust fail loud. Fixed input → fixed expected hash; never goes stale.
